author: OfficialLoganK
text:
Excited to share that the Gemini API now has prepaid billing, rolled out to start for US customers!!

We have been working hard across Google to enable this. It’s the default for new API users and existing users can opt in via a new billing account, all directly in AI Studio.

author: OfficialLoganK
text:
they are charged at whatever you set in the request (unless your priority is downgraded).

my suggestion is that you would setup your 503 retry logic in a cascading fashion, if a request fails with flex, switch to default, if it fails with default and it’s high priority, switch ti priority

author: OfficialLoganK
text: Will these apply only in peak demand scenarios or always? If there is no peak in demand (can serve 'normally') and user uses flex or priority: will be processed as normal and pay regular?  Or will be always charged at the flex / priority prices?

author: OfficialLoganK
text:
Today we are rolling out service tiers in the Gemini API! You can now (optionally) set "flex" or "priority".

In the case of flex, this will save you ~50% on API costs (with lower reliability).

In the case of priority, this will cost ~80% more but give you higher priority!

Gemma 4 is build to run on your hardware: phones, laptops, and desktops.

Frontier intelligence with a 26B MOE and a 31B Dense model!

None of the current systems / norms are setup for this world yet.

author: OfficialLoganK
text: Fix coming soon! We are adding more controls to these deployments which should land this week.

author: OfficialLoganK
text:
There are two levers here in the Gemini API: billing account caps and user set spend caps.

For billing account caps, starting in April, each Tier (1-3) will have a max spending cap set. Once you hit that limit, you cannot spend more (upper bound of spending).

For user set spend

author: OfficialLoganK
text: 503's are the only tough situation here, the update from today should make getting less 429's much easier. For 503's we are making TPU's as fast as possible but there are certain times of day / week when we hit global capacity spikes which causes us to have to shed traffic.

author: OfficialLoganK
text: it takes ~10 minutes for the caps to go into effect, once they are all requests are blocked

author: OfficialLoganK
text: yeah we didn't do automatic to start since they used to serve as glorified cost caps, but not that we have default billing account caps and user set spend caps, we can now upgrade folks automatically!

author: OfficialLoganK
text: Plus we rolled out spend caps last week to let you set an API budget, a bunch of new rate limit + billing + usage dashboards to make monitoring costs easier, and more!

author: OfficialLoganK
text:
We just shipped a bunch of stuff to make it easier to scale with the Gemini API:

- Automatic tier upgrades
- Tier 1 -> Tier 2 now happens much faster (30 days post payment -> 3 days) and with less spend ($250 -> $100)
- New billing account caps on each tier to limit over spend
